message:
Fix Notify baton-passing on cancelled futures with pending notifications

When a Notified future that has been marked as notified (via notify_one)
is cancelled (dropped or observes a generation change), the notification
token was being lost — no subsequent waiter would receive it. This
caused notify_one signals to be silently swallowed when the designated
recipient was cancelled before consuming the notification.

The fix checks whether the waiter being removed had been individually
notified (was_notified && notified_generation matches initial_generation)
before dropping the lock. If so, instead of dropping the waiters lock
directly, it calls pass_baton() to transfer the notification to the next
eligible waiter in the queue. This applies to both the optimistic
(lock-free generation check) and pessimistic (under-lock re-check) code
paths in Notified::poll.

Also updates the cancel-safety integration test (tests/notify_bug.rs)
to validate commutativity: the test now asserts that polling a notified
future and dropping a notified future produce the same baton-passing
behavior, using assert_eq! instead of separate assert! calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
